evals.prompt: Document our authentication system end to end — I want the full doc set: something for new users to learn from, task guides, reference material, and the why behind it, all cross-linked.
evals.expected_output: Selects the diataxis recipe, decomposes the subject into tutorial/how-to/reference/explanation, fans out to those four genre skills, and reconciles relates-to cross-links across them.
expectations: Explains why a single document wouldn't serve all four user needs, justifying the doc-set approach over a single genre artifact
Confirms the cross-document relationships[] graph is link-complete before considering the set done

evals.prompt: Produce the Kiro spec set for the new export feature.
evals.expected_output: Uses the kiro recipe to produce kiro-requirements, kiro-design, and kiro-tasks in sequence, with each document derived-from the one before it.

deterministic_checks.description: uses the recipe's derived-from cross-link type

expectations: Selects the kiro recipe rather than a generic multi-document plan
Sequences the three documents so design is grounded in requirements and tasks are grounded in design, not produced independently

evals.prompt: I just need a single ADR for choosing Postgres. Do I need the planner?
evals.expected_output: Explains ADR is a standalone singleton invoked directly, not decomposed via a doc-set recipe.

deterministic_checks.description: recommends the adr skill by name
names it a singleton, not a doc-set
does not describe fanning out to member skills for a singleton

expectations: States clearly that this request does not require the planner
Explains that a single ADR has no cross-linked member set, unlike a doc-set recipe

evals.prompt: We need the full AI-ready spec channel for our new billing engine — problem statement, the solution spec, and the architecture with NFRs, all linked.
evals.expected_output: Selects the ai-spec recipe: prd, feature-spec, and ai-architecture-doc, with the PRD realized-by the feature spec.

deterministic_checks.description: uses the recipe's realized-by cross-link type

expectations: Selects the ai-spec recipe rather than diataxis or kiro
Explains the problem -> solution -> architecture decomposition order and that the feature spec depends on the architecture doc

evals.prompt: Produce a full architecture doc set for our payments service — the narrative plus the diagrams, kept in sync.
evals.expected_output: Selects the architecture recipe: an arc42-arch-doc narrative plus c4-model-diagram, cross-linked via relates-to.

deterministic_checks.description: uses the recipe's relates-to cross-link type
describes the arc42 doc as the narrative half of the pair

expectations: Selects the architecture recipe rather than a single arc42 document
Explains that the diagrams and narrative should reference the same architectural elements, not be produced in isolation

evals.prompt: I ran the doc-set planner for our export feature's Kiro set, but tasks.md references design section 4.2 and design.md only has sections 1-3. Is that a problem?
evals.expected_output: Flags this as a link-completeness failure in the kiro recipe's cross-link contract — a dangling reference means the set is not link-complete — and recommends a concrete fix rather than shipping as-is.

deterministic_checks.description: names the failure as a link-completeness problem
does not dismiss the dangling reference
addresses the specific dangling section reference
identifies the design document as the source of the gap

expectations: Identifies the dangling reference as a link-completeness failure specific to this recipe's cross-link contract
Recommends a concrete fix (add the missing design section or correct the reference) rather than dismissing the mismatch
